title: Project Pulse
summary: Issue #56 has moved forward again: route activation now covers channel, forum, direct, and thread surfaces, the client shows manifest-backed surface and helper-slot state in the scope summary, issue #57 remains in progress, and the project board is still GraphQL-blocked.
source: GitHub-first NEXUS truth sweep during the active issue #56 surface-expansion lane, plus verified local implementation coverage for widened route activation and client-visible activation state.
nextAction: Publish the widened issue #56 surface-expansion delta, keep helper-slot and activation diagnostics legible in the client, and retry project-board sync when GitHub GraphQL recovers.

lanes.title: Project Pulse operator surface
lanes.summary: The right-rail Project Pulse and readable runtime-health surface shipped to main and now anchor operator-visible progress and readiness in the client.
lanes.nextAction: Keep the snapshot truthful as active NEXUS lanes move.

lanes.title: Desktop readiness validation
lanes.summary: Continuity validation closed cleanly on the Electron and Node baseline, and the merged repo remains green with 75 out of 75 automated checks after the later route-activation coverage landed.
lanes.nextAction: Use the verified baseline as the migration source for the next runtime-topology work.

lanes.title: README direction clarification
lanes.summary: The README now explicitly distinguishes the current Electron continuity baseline from the approved native-C plus Avalonia target direction.
lanes.nextAction: Keep future topology work out of the README lane unless operator-facing baseline wording changes again.

lanes.title: First runtime-topology slice
lanes.summary: The first concrete runtime-topology record shipped to main with the native runtime core, Avalonia host, package boundaries, and interop contract made explicit.
lanes.nextAction: Turn the topology record into manifest shapes and migration-boundary decisions.

lanes.title: Runtime package manifest contracts
lanes.summary: The first explicit surface-package and helper-package manifest contracts are merged on main, and Project Pulse can now expose architecture artifacts directly in the UI.
lanes.nextAction: Use the merged contracts to pick the first migration seam and activate the conversation-surface program model.

lanes.title: Manifest-backed route activation for conversation surfaces
lanes.summary: The service runtime adapter now activates thread routes through the manifest registry, resolves helper slots with explicit degradation semantics, and exposes the first additive route-activation API without breaking the current HTTP contract.
lanes.nextAction: Build on the merged activation seam by widening surface coverage and surfacing activation state in the operator-facing client.

lanes.title: Degraded runtime supervisor seam
lanes.summary: The service now keeps running when supervisor startup fails, exposes structured readiness and failure state through `/api/health`, and returns clean `503` responses for runtime-only activation when the supervisor is not ready.
lanes.nextAction: Use the merged supervisor seam to widen operator-visible runtime state and continue the next conversation-surface expansion lane.

lanes.title: Packaging and runtime topology
lanes.summary: Native-C-first topology remains active, and the degraded runtime supervisor seam is now live in code. Deeper runtime ownership and project-board reconciliation still remain before this lane is complete.
lanes.nextAction: Retry project-board sync when GitHub GraphQL recovers, then keep moving activation and lifecycle ownership behind the runtime seam without breaking continuity.

lanes.title: Conversation-surface program model
lanes.summary: Manifest-backed route activation now spans thread, channel, forum, and direct surfaces in the active implementation lane, and the operator-facing client now exposes surface-program, helper-slot, and activation-diagnostic state directly in the scope summary.
lanes.nextAction: Publish the current surface-expansion delta, then continue with deeper helper-slot registry behavior and richer operator-visible activation state.

lanes.title: ASCENT shared-surface placeholder
lanes.summary: Reserved integration space only. This remains intentionally parked and should not pull focus away from the current continuity and runtime lanes.
lanes.nextAction: Leave parked until the active desktop and conversation-surface lanes warrant reopening it.

lanes.title: ASCENT notification placeholder
lanes.summary: Reserved notification integration space only. It stays parked as a placeholder rather than active build work.
lanes.nextAction: Keep parked until the current NEXUS execution lanes are materially further along.
